behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive watch coverage). MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: a mocked event source can claim subdirectory events exist even when OS watcher config misses them. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff/state boundary: request intake -> dedupe window store -> pipeline enqueue. In-process wiring with fake clock catches real bugs in TTL/state propagation without requiring OS events. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: text says 'processing request' (not OS event semantics), so real watcher infra is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire daemon intake + dedupe component + enqueue path with an in-memory queue and controllable clock; submit same file path twice inside window and assert only first reaches pipeline, then advance clock past window and assert next request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS event duplication ('operating system emits multiple filesystem events'). MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher often emits idealized events and can miss duplicate-event races/debounce bugs. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher on a real filesystem, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename or rapid append), and assert one pipeline execution/output for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS watcher lifecycle across real delete/recreate operations. In-process fakes usually do not reproduce handle invalidation/rebind behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML component, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real file metadata/timestamp resolution plus concurrent arrival events. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed timestamps/events can hide filesystem-specific ordering bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML output or multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously on real filesystem with controlled creation times, run daemon, and assert processing order matches observed creation timestamps (including tie-break rule if implemented).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure requires real write-in-progress timing and watcher event timing; fakes commonly miss partial-write races. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both point to System. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, open a file and write in chunks with delays (keeping stream open), trigger arrival event naturally, assert processing does not start until writer closes and final content is then processed once.
